Question: Verify that $$y = 2 e^{3 x} - 2 x - 2$$ is a solution to the differential equation $$y^{\prime} - 3 y = 6 x + 4 .$$
What is the order of the following differential equation? $$( x^{4} - 3 x ) y^{( 5 )} - ( 3 x^{2} + 1 ) y^{\prime} + 3 y = \text{sin} x \text{cos} x$$

Find the particular solution to the differential equation $$y^{\prime} = 4 x + 3$$ passing through the point $$( 1 , 7 ) ,$$ given that $$y = 2 x^{2} + 3 x + C$$ is a general solution to the differential equation.
Verify that $$y = 3 e^{2 t} + 4 \text{sin} t$$ is a solution to the initial-value problem $$y^{\prime} - 2 y = 4 \text{cos} t - 8 \text{sin} t , y ( 0 ) = 3 .$$
Solve the initial-value problem $$y^{\prime} = x^{2} - 4 x + 3 - 6 e^{x} , y ( 0 ) = 8 .$$

Suppose a rock falls from rest from a height of $$100$$ meters and the only force acting on it is gravity. Find an equation for the velocity $$v ( t )$$ as a function of time, measured in meters per second.
